texto: I am oblique; I think that has to do with my own nature. I like trying to do things which work on many levels, because I think it is terribly important to give an audience a lot of things they might not get as well as those they will, so that finally the film does take on a texture and is not just simplistic communication.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Alan J. Pakula: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “I am oblique; I think that has to do with my own nature. I like trying to do things which work on…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, work, nature pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e nature.

I like trying to do things which work on many levels, because I think it is terribly important to give an audience a lot of things they might not get as well as those they will, so that finally the film does take on a texture and is not just simplistic communication.
